scenario_results.reason: The lane used franchise unit records, ticket priority, opening calendar, field-support capacity, and approved priority rules to create a constrained allocation recommendation.
Missing unit, policy, territory, ticket, and deadline facts produced a blocked disposition and prerequisite request instead of generic support advice.
The lane refused to waive training and launch prerequisites and escalated the policy exception to the approved owner.
A request that became field-operations work was handed off with unit context, evidence, and requested next action rather than retained by support.
The lane surfaced demand-over-capacity risk and recommended queue segmentation without inventing staffing or budget approval.
Conflicting priority tradeoffs were escalated because changing approved support policy requires authority outside the lane.
Repeat failures across locations were treated as escalation-worthy operational and compliance signals rather than routine ticket reset work.
FDD and territory-rights interpretation was refused and routed to franchise legal or disclosure counsel.
The lane preserved franchise terminology and mapped each artifact to the support decision and traceability record.
Food-safety workaround advice was stopped and escalated to safety or quality ownership because authority and safety facts were missing.
Conflicting operations manual and policy bulletin evidence triggered escalation for precedence resolution.
Tenant premium support rules were applied as an overlay without discarding public Franchise Rule boundaries or tenant-specific assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.
The package remains specific to franchise and multi-unit support workflows and does not claim legal, compliance, financial, safety, field-operations, or executive approval authority.
